---
title: "Car ownership investigation"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
library(haven)        # Read SPSS files
library(dplyr)        # Data manipulation
library(ggplot2)      # Visualizations
library(tidyr)        # Data reshaping
library(scales)       # Scale functions for plots
library(forcats)      # Factor manipulation
library(extrafont)

current_dir <- getwd()
source(file.path(current_dir,"src","R","utils.R"))

# Define years
years <- c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2025)
#years <- c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023 )


waves <- list()
for (year in years) {
  file_path <- paste0("/home/amy/tmp/sheds_data/files/SHEDS", year, ".sav")
  wave_name <- as.character(year)   # "2025" not "w2025"
  waves[[wave_name]] <- read_clean_sheds(file_path)
}

car_history <- build_car_history_stata(waves)

car_history %>%
  filter(year_wave == 2025) %>%
  summarise(
    original_na = sum(is.na(mob3_3)),
    filled_na   = sum(is.na(mob3_3_filled)),
    recovered   = sum(is.na(mob3_3)) - sum(is.na(mob3_3_filled))
  ) %>%
  print()

car_history_2025 <- car_history %>%
  filter(year_wave == 2025) %>%
  select(id, mob3_3_filled, mob2_e_filled)

print(car_history_2025)

car_data <- waves[["2025"]] %>%
  left_join(car_history_2025, by = "id") %>%
  mutate(
    car_owner = case_when(
      mob2_1 == 0  ~ "No car",
      mob2_1 >= 1  ~ "Car owner",
      TRUE         ~ NA_character_
    ),
    num_cars = mob2_1,

    # Raw engine type (only answered on car change year)
    engine_type_raw = case_when(
      mob3_3 == 1 ~ "Gasoline",
      mob3_3 == 2 ~ "Diesel",
      mob3_3 == 6 ~ "Hybrid gasoline",
      mob3_3 == 7 ~ "Hybrid diesel",
      mob3_3 == 8 ~ "Electric",
      mob3_3 == 3 ~ "Natural gas",
      mob3_3 == 9 ~ "Other",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # Forward-filled engine type (from build_car_history)
    engine_type = case_when(
      mob3_3_filled == 1 ~ "Gasoline",
      mob3_3_filled == 2 ~ "Diesel",
      mob3_3_filled == 6 ~ "Hybrid gasoline",
      mob3_3_filled == 7 ~ "Hybrid diesel",
      mob3_3_filled == 8 ~ "Electric",
      mob3_3_filled == 3 ~ "Natural gas",
      mob3_3_filled == 9 ~ "Other",
      TRUE               ~ NA_character_
    ),

    age_group = case_when(
      agegr == 1 ~ "18-34",
      agegr == 2 ~ "35-54",
      agegr == 3 ~ "55+",
      TRUE       ~ NA_character_
    ),

    gender = case_when(
      sex == 0 ~ "Male",
      sex == 1 ~ "Female",
      sex == 2 ~ "Other",
      TRUE     ~ NA_character_
    ),

    region_label = case_when(
      region == 1 ~ "Suisse romande",
      region == 2 ~ "Alpen und\nVoralpen",
      region == 3 ~ "Westmittelland",
      region == 4 ~ "Ostmittelland",
      region == 5 ~ "Ticino",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ),

    living_area = case_when(
      md_wohntyp3_alt == 1 ~ "City",
      md_wohntyp3_alt == 2 ~ "Agglomeration",
      md_wohntyp3_alt == 3 ~ "Countryside",
      TRUE                 ~ NA_character_
    ),

    residence_type = case_when(
      residtype == 1 ~ "Tenant",
      residtype == 2 ~ "Owner",
      TRUE           ~ NA_character_
    ),

    income_level = case_when(
      seco5 ==  1 ~ "≤3,000",
      seco5 ==  2 ~ "3,000-4,499",
      seco5 ==  3 ~ "4,500-5,999",
      seco5 ==  4 ~ "6,000-8,999",
      seco5 ==  5 ~ "9,000-12,000",
      seco5 ==  6 ~ ">12,000",
      seco5 == -3 ~ "Prefer not to say",
      seco5 == -1 ~ "Don't know",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ),

    household_size = rowSums(select(., starts_with("seco1b_")), na.rm = TRUE),

    annual_km = case_when(
      mob7a ==  1 ~ "≤5,000 km",
      mob7a ==  2 ~ "5,001-10,000 km",
      mob7a ==  3 ~ "10,001-15,000 km",
      mob7a ==  4 ~ "15,001-20,000 km",
      mob7a ==  5 ~ "20,001-25,000 km",
      mob7a ==  6 ~ "25,001-30,000 km",
      mob7a ==  7 ~ "30,001-35,000 km",
      mob7a ==  8 ~ "35,001-40,000 km",
      mob7a ==  9 ~ "40,001-45,000 km",
      mob7a == 10 ~ "45,001-50,000 km",
      mob7a == 11 ~ ">50,000 km",
      mob7a == -1 ~ "Don't know",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    ),

    distance_cat = case_when(
      mob7a %in% c(1, 2)                ~ "Low\n(≤10,000 km/year)",
      mob7a %in% c(3, 4, 5)             ~ "Medium\n(10,000-25,000 km/year)",
      mob7a %in% c(6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11) ~ "High\n(>25,000 km/year)",
      mob7a == -1                        ~ "Unknown",
      TRUE                               ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    age_group    = factor(age_group,    levels = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+")),
    income_level = factor(income_level, levels = c("≤3,000", "3,000-4,499",
                                                    "4,500-5,999", "6,000-8,999",
                                                    "9,000-12,000", ">12,000",
                                                    "Don't know", "Prefer not to say")),
    distance_cat = factor(distance_cat, levels = c("Low\n(≤10,000 km/year)",
                                                    "Medium\n(10,000-25,000 km/year)",
                                                    "High\n(>25,000 km/year)",
                                                    "Unknown"))
  )


```

## ============================================================
## DEMOGRAPHIC VARIABLES CONFIGURATION
## ============================================================
## CONTROL ALL DEMOGRAPHIC VARIABLES AND LABELS HERE
## ============================================================

```{r}
# Define which demographics to include (TRUE/FALSE)
DEMO_CONFIG <- list(
  age_group    = list(include = TRUE,  label = "Age Group"),
  gender       = list(include = FALSE, label = "Gender"),      # <-- Set to FALSE to exclude
  living_area  = list(include = TRUE,  label = "Living Area"),
  income       = list(include = TRUE,  label = "Income Level")
)

# Category orders and labels (controls bar sequence left-to-right)
CATEGORY_CONFIG <- list(
  age_group = list(
    levels = c("18-34", "35-54", "55+")
  ),
  gender = list(
    levels = c("Male", "Female")
  ),
  living_area = list(
    levels = c("City", "Agglomeration", "Countryside")
  ),
  income = list(
    levels = c("Low (≤4,500)", "Medium (4,500-9,000)", "High (>9,000)")
  )
)

# Regression term labels (for LaTeX tables and forest plots)
REGRESSION_LABELS <- list(
  age_group = list(
    "age_group35-54" = "Age: 35--54",
    "age_group55+"   = "Age: 55+"
  ),
  gender = list(
    "genderFemale" = "Gender: Female"
  ),
  living_area = list(
    "living_areaAgglomeration" = "Area: Agglomeration",
    "living_areaCountryside"   = "Area: Countryside"
  ),
  income = list(
    "income_catMedium (4,500-9,000)" = "Income: Medium (4,500--9,000)",
    "income_catHigh (>9,000)"        = "Income: High ($>$9,000)"
  )
)

# Short labels for forest plots
REGRESSION_LABELS_SHORT <- list(
  age_group = list(
    "age_group35-54" = "Age: 35–54",
    "age_group55+"   = "Age: 55+"
  ),
  gender = list(
    "genderFemale" = "Gender: Female"
  ),
  living_area = list(
    "living_areaAgglomeration" = "Area: Agglomeration",
    "living_areaCountryside"   = "Area: Countryside"
  ),
  income = list(
    "income_catMedium (4,500-9,000)" = "Income: Medium",
    "income_catHigh (>9,000)"        = "Income: High"
  )
)

# Helper function to recode income
recode_income <- function(x) case_when(
  x %in% c("≤3,000", "3,000-4,499")      ~ "Low (≤4,500)",
  x %in% c("4,500-5,999", "6,000-8,999") ~ "Medium (4,500-9,000)",
  x %in% c("9,000-12,000", ">12,000")    ~ "High (>9,000)",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)

# Get included demographics
INCLUDED_DEMOS <- names(DEMO_CONFIG)[sapply(DEMO_CONFIG, function(x) x$include)]

# Print configuration
cat("\n=== Analysis Configuration ===\n")
for (demo in INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ %s\n", DEMO_CONFIG[[demo]]$label))
}
cat("\n")
```

check:
```{r}
valid_car    <- validate_car_history(car_history, "car_history_forward", waves)
```

Demographics:
```{r}
create_crosstab <- function(data, group_var) {
  data %>%
    filter(!is.na(!!sym(group_var)), !is.na(car_owner)) %>%
    group_by(!!sym(group_var), car_owner) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(!!sym(group_var)) %>%
    mutate(
      total = sum(n),
      percentage = n / total * 100
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
}

car_by_age <- if ("age_group" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) create_crosstab(car_data, "age_group") else NULL
car_by_gender <- if ("gender" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) create_crosstab(car_data, "gender") %>% filter(gender != "Other") else NULL
car_by_region <- create_crosstab(car_data, "region_label")
car_by_area <- if ("living_area" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) create_crosstab(car_data, "living_area") else NULL

car_by_income <- if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  car_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(income_level),
           !income_level %in% c("Don't know", "Prefer not to say"),
           !is.na(car_owner)) %>%
    group_by(income_level, car_owner) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    group_by(income_level) %>%
    mutate(
      total = sum(n),
      percentage = n / total * 100
    )
} else NULL


unique(car_by_area$living_area)

income_simple_full <- if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  car_by_income %>%
    mutate(income_cat = recode_income(income_level)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(income_cat)) %>%
    group_by(income_cat, car_owner) %>%
    summarise(
      n = sum(n),
      total = sum(total),
      percentage = n / total * 100,
      .groups = "drop"
    )
} else NULL

# Build combined data from included demographics
combined_parts <- list()
if ("age_group" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  combined_parts[[length(combined_parts) + 1]] <- car_by_age %>%
    mutate(demographic = DEMO_CONFIG$age_group$label,
           category = as.character(age_group))
}
if ("living_area" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  combined_parts[[length(combined_parts) + 1]] <- car_by_area %>%
    mutate(demographic = DEMO_CONFIG$living_area$label,
           category = as.character(living_area))
}
if ("gender" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  combined_parts[[length(combined_parts) + 1]] <- car_by_gender %>%
    mutate(demographic = DEMO_CONFIG$gender$label,
           category = as.character(gender))
}
if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  combined_parts[[length(combined_parts) + 1]] <- income_simple_full %>%
    mutate(demographic = DEMO_CONFIG$income$label,
           category = as.character(income_cat))
}

combined_data_full <- bind_rows(combined_parts) %>%
  mutate(
    demographic = factor(demographic, 
                        levels = sapply(DEMO_CONFIG[INCLUDED_DEMOS], function(x) x$label)),
    category = factor(category,
                      levels = unlist(sapply(INCLUDED_DEMOS, function(d) CATEGORY_CONFIG[[d]]$levels))),
    car_owner = factor(car_owner, levels = c("Car owner", "No car"))
  )

# Stacked bar version
p_combined_stacked <- ggplot(combined_data_full,
                             aes(x = category, y = percentage, fill = car_owner)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Car owner" = "#4A90A4", "No car" = "#D4634E")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  facet_wrap(~ demographic, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
  labs(
   # title = "Car Ownership Rates Across Different Demographics",
   # subtitle = "Distribution of car owners and non-car owners by demographic group",
    x = "",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  theme_publication()+
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 15),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 15)
  )

print(p_combined_stacked)

save_plot(p_combined_stacked,"car_ownership_combined_stacked",width = 12, height = 8)
```


EV adoption combined:
```{r}

ev_by_distance <- car_data %>%
  filter(car_owner == "Car owner",
         !is.na(engine_type),
         !is.na(distance_cat),
         distance_cat != "Unknown") %>%
  mutate(vehicle_type = ifelse(engine_type == "Electric" | mob2_e_filled == 1, "Electric", "Non-Electric")) %>%
  group_by(distance_cat, vehicle_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(distance_cat) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percentage = n / total * 100
  )

# EV data by living area
ev_by_area <- car_data %>%
  filter(car_owner == "Car owner", !is.na(engine_type), !is.na(living_area)) %>%
  mutate(vehicle_type = ifelse(engine_type == "Electric" | mob2_e_filled == 1, "Electric", "Non-Electric")) %>%
  group_by(living_area, vehicle_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(living_area) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percentage = n / total * 100
  )

ev_by_age <- car_data %>%
  filter(car_owner == "Car owner", !is.na(engine_type), !is.na(age_group)) %>%
  mutate(vehicle_type = ifelse(engine_type == "Electric" | mob2_e_filled == 1, "Electric", "Non-Electric")) %>%
  group_by(age_group, vehicle_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(age_group) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percentage = n / total * 100
  )

# By income
ev_by_income_simple <- car_data %>%
  filter(car_owner == "Car owner",
         !is.na(engine_type),
         !is.na(income_level),
         !income_level %in% c("Don't know", "Prefer not to say")) %>%
  mutate(
    vehicle_type = ifelse(engine_type == "Electric"| mob2_e_filled == 1, "Electric", "Non-Electric"),
    income_cat = case_when(
      income_level %in% c("≤3,000", "3,000-4,499") ~ "Low\n(≤4,500)",
      income_level %in% c("4,500-5,999", "6,000-8,999") ~ "Medium\n(4,500-9,000)",
      income_level %in% c("9,000-12,000", ">12,000") ~ "High\n(>9,000)"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(income_cat)) %>%
  group_by(income_cat, vehicle_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(income_cat) %>%
  mutate(
    total = sum(n),
    percentage = n / total * 100,
    income_cat = factor(income_cat,
                       levels = c("Low\n(≤4,500)",
                                 "Medium\n(4,500-9,000)",
                                 "High\n(>9,000)"))
  )

engine_simple <- car_data %>%
  filter(car_owner == "Car owner",
         !is.na(engine_type),
         !is.na(income_level),
         !income_level %in% c("Don't know", "Prefer not to say")) %>%
  mutate(
    is_electric = ifelse(engine_type == "Electric", "Electric", "Non-electric")
  ) %>%
  group_by(income_level, is_electric) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(income_level) %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100)

ev_by_income <- engine_simple %>%
  group_by(income_level, is_electric) %>%
  summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(income_level) %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  filter(is_electric == "Electric")
print(ev_by_income)

unique(ev_by_income_simple$income_cat)

combined_ev_data <- bind_rows(
  ev_by_age %>% ungroup() %>%
    mutate(demographic = "Age Group",
           category = as.character(age_group)) %>%
    select(demographic, category, vehicle_type, percentage),

  ev_by_area %>% ungroup() %>%
    mutate(demographic = "Living Area",
           category = as.character(living_area)) %>%
    select(demographic, category, vehicle_type, percentage),

  ev_by_distance %>% ungroup() %>%
    mutate(demographic = "Distance Traveled",
           category = gsub("\n", " ", as.character(distance_cat))) %>%
    select(demographic, category, vehicle_type, percentage),

  ev_by_income_simple %>% ungroup() %>%
    mutate(demographic = "Income Level",
           category = gsub("\n", " ", as.character(income_cat))) %>%
    select(demographic, category, vehicle_type, percentage)
) %>%
  filter(category != "Unknown") %>%
  select(demographic, category, vehicle_type, percentage) %>%
  mutate(
    demographic  = factor(demographic,
                          levels = c("Age Group", "Living Area",
                                     "Distance Traveled", "Income Level")),
    vehicle_type = factor(vehicle_type, levels = c("Non-Electric", "Electric")),
    category     = factor(category, levels = c(
      "18-34", "35-54", "55+",
      "City", "Agglomeration", "Countryside",
      "Low (≤10,000 km/year)", "Medium (10,000-25,000 km/year)", "High (>25,000 km/year)",
      "Low (≤4,500)", "Medium (4,500-9,000)", "High (>9,000)"
    ))
  )

# Find the dropped row
cat("NA categories:\n")
print(combined_ev_data %>% filter(is.na(category)))


# Define colors for both vehicle types
ev_colors <- c("Electric" = "#2ECC71", "Non-Electric" = "#95A5A6")

print(combined_ev_data)

# Combined stacked bar plot
p11_combined <- ggplot(combined_ev_data,
                       aes(x = category, y = percentage, fill = vehicle_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ev_colors,
                    breaks = c("Electric", "Non-Electric")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 102), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  facet_wrap(~ demographic, scales = "free_x", nrow = 1) +
  labs(
    #title = "Electric Vehicle Adoption Across Demographics",
    #subtitle = "Percentage of electric vs non-electric vehicles among car owners",
    x = "",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  theme_publication(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16)
  )

print(p11_combined)

save_plot(p11_combined, "ev_adoption_combined", width=12, height = 8 )
```


```{r}

ev_by_age %>% filter(age_group == "35-54")

```

Multinomial logit for no car/ not electric car / electric:

```{r}
library(nnet)
table(car_data$car_owner)

multi_data <- car_data %>%
  mutate(
   vehicle_class = case_when(
     car_owner == "No car"                                              ~ "No car",
     engine_type == "Electric" | mob2_e_filled == 1                   ~ "Electric",
     engine_type %in% c("Gasoline", "Diesel", "Hybrid gasoline",
                     "Hybrid diesel", "Natural gas", "Other")       ~ "Non-Electric",
     TRUE ~ NA_character_
   ),
    income_cat = recode_income(income_level),
    distance_simple = case_when(
      mob7a %in% c(1, 2)                ~ "Low",
      mob7a %in% c(3, 4, 5)             ~ "Medium",
      mob7a %in% c(6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11) ~ "High",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(vehicle_class))

# Filter based on configuration
if ("age_group" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  multi_data <- multi_data %>% filter(!is.na(age_group))
  multi_data$age_group <- factor(multi_data$age_group, levels = CATEGORY_CONFIG$age_group$levels)
}
if ("gender" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  multi_data <- multi_data %>% filter(!is.na(gender), gender != "Other")
  multi_data$gender <- factor(multi_data$gender, levels = CATEGORY_CONFIG$gender$levels)
}
if ("living_area" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  multi_data <- multi_data %>% filter(!is.na(living_area))
  multi_data$living_area <- factor(multi_data$living_area, levels = CATEGORY_CONFIG$living_area$levels)
}
if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  multi_data <- multi_data %>% filter(!is.na(income_cat))
  multi_data$income_cat <- factor(multi_data$income_cat, levels = CATEGORY_CONFIG$income$levels)
}

multi_data$vehicle_class <- factor(multi_data$vehicle_class, levels = c("No car", "Non-Electric", "Electric"))

cat("Outcome distribution (full sample):\n")
print(table(multi_data$vehicle_class))

# Build formula
formula_terms <- c()
if ("age_group" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) formula_terms <- c(formula_terms, "age_group")
if ("gender" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) formula_terms <- c(formula_terms, "gender")
if ("living_area" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) formula_terms <- c(formula_terms, "living_area")
if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) formula_terms <- c(formula_terms, "income_cat")

formula_str <- paste("vehicle_class ~", paste(formula_terms, collapse = " + "))
m1 <- multinom(
  as.formula(formula_str),
  data  = multi_data,
  trace = FALSE
)

summary(m1)

m1_tbl <- tidy(m1, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  rename(Outcome = y.level, OR = estimate, SE = std.error,
         z = statistic, p = p.value, CI_low = conf.low, CI_high = conf.high) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3)))

print(m1_tbl)

null_m1 <- multinom(vehicle_class ~ 1, data = multi_data, trace = FALSE)
cat("McFadden R2 (Model 1):", round(1 - logLik(m1) / logLik(null_m1), 3), "\n")
```

Predictied probability table:
```{r}
library(broom)
library(knitr)

# Get z-scores and p-values manually
z_scores <- summary(m1)$coefficients / summary(m1)$standard.errors
p_values  <- (1 - pnorm(abs(z_scores))) * 2

term_recode <- unlist(lapply(INCLUDED_DEMOS, function(d) REGRESSION_LABELS[[d]]))

tidy(m1, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term, !!!term_recode),
    sig = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE            ~ ""
    ),
    OR = paste0(round(estimate, 3), sig),
    CI = paste0("[", round(conf.low, 3), ", ", round(conf.high, 3), "]")
  ) %>%
  select(y.level, term, OR, CI) %>%
  rename(Outcome = y.level, Predictor = term) %>%
  kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
        caption = "Multinomial Logit: Vehicle Class (ref = No car)",
        label   = "tab:multinom") %>%
  cat(file = "MultinomCarTable.tex")

term_recode_short <- unlist(lapply(INCLUDED_DEMOS, function(d) REGRESSION_LABELS_SHORT[[d]]))

plot <- tidy(m1, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term, !!!term_recode_short),
    term = factor(term, levels = rev(unlist(lapply(INCLUDED_DEMOS, function(d) REGRESSION_LABELS_SHORT[[d]])))),
    y.level = factor(y.level, levels = c("Non-Electric", "Electric")),
    stars = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "***",
            ifelse(p.value < 0.01,  "**",
            ifelse(p.value < 0.05,  "*", "")))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = y.level)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high),
                 height = 0.25, linewidth = 1.2,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +

  geom_point(size = 3.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_text(aes(x = conf.high, label = stars),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
            hjust = -0.3, vjust = 0.5, size = 6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Non-Electric" = "#95A5A6",
                                "Electric"     = "#2ECC71")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_publication() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(plot)

save_plot(plot,"multinom_reg_car_demographics")

library(marginaleffects)

# Average predicted probabilities by income (if included)
if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  predictions(m1,
              newdata = datagrid(income_cat = levels(multi_data$income_cat))) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    select(group, income_cat, estimate, conf.low, conf.high) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 3))) %>%
    kable(caption = "Predicted probabilities by income group")
}


```



Check results:
```{r}
cat("Total respondents:", nrow(car_data), "\n")
cat("car_owner:\n"); print(table(car_data$car_owner, useNA = "always"))

car_owners <- car_data %>% filter(car_owner == "Car owner")
cat("N =", nrow(car_owners), "\n")

cat("Has engine_type:", sum(!is.na(car_owners$engine_type)), "\n")
cat("Missing engine_type:", sum(is.na(car_owners$engine_type)),
    paste0("(", round(mean(is.na(car_owners$engine_type)) * 100, 1), "% of car owners)\n"))
cat("engine_type breakdown:\n")
print(table(car_owners$engine_type, useNA = "always"))

cat("\n=== Missing data for included variables ===\n")
car_with_engine <- car_owners %>% filter(!is.na(engine_type))
if ("age_group" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  cat("Missing age_group:   ", sum(is.na(car_with_engine$age_group)), "\n")
}
if ("gender" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  cat("Missing gender:      ", sum(is.na(car_with_engine$gender)),
      "| 'Other':", sum(car_with_engine$gender == "Other", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
}
if ("living_area" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  cat("Missing living_area: ", sum(is.na(car_with_engine$living_area)), "\n")
}
if ("income" %in% INCLUDED_DEMOS) {
  cat("Missing income_level:", sum(is.na(car_with_engine$income_level)), "\n")
}
cat("Missing distance (mob7a):", sum(is.na(car_with_engine$mob7a)), "\n")
```
